commands.review_summary:
The PR tightens cron automatic-delivery prompting, filters compact self-narrating text-only cron delivery payloads before outbound send, and adds focused cron delivery regression tests.

PR surface: Source +46, Tests +71. Total +117 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. with high-confidence source and proof evidence: current main sends normalized cron payload text through direct delivery, and the Mantis baseline shows the self-narrating Telegram bubble. I did not run a local current-main repro, so this is source/proof-backed rather than locally reproduced.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed; this PR is ready for ordinary maintainer review and CI with the message-delivery heuristic risk visible.

target.title: fix(cron): block self-narrating auto-announce replies
target.body:
## Summary
- tighten cron auto-delivery instructions so automatic final answers must be the exact user-facing message, not a narration of what was sent
- add a delivery-side guard that blocks compact self-narrating cron auto-announce text before it reaches outbound delivery
- keep substantive user-facing messages and multi-line content deliverable

## Testing
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts`
- Result: 2 files passed, 45 tests passed

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Cron auto-announce delivery should not send compact self-narrating final text such as a message that says the assistant already sent or asked the user something. It should block that internal/meta narration before outbound delivery and still allow substantive user-facing messages.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at this PR head on macOS, using the repository-installed dependencies and the actual cron isolated-agent delivery dispatch code path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw checkout after applying this patch:

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/jeremy-builder/Building/OpenClaw

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  45 passed (45)
Duration  5.20s
```

- Observed result after fix: The delivery regression exercises the cron direct-delivery path with a compact self-narrating final payload and observes `delivered === false`, `deliveryAttempted === true`, and no Telegram send call. The helper regression also observes that a substantive user-facing lunch-options style message is not flagged.
- What was not tested: No live Telegram bot/group end-to-end run was performed for this PR; the proof is from the local OpenClaw runtime test harness and focused dispatch-path regression.
